study_group_representative.quote: characteristics of 7755 patients with COVID-19 in South Korea as of 13 March 2020 using surveillance data retrieved from the KCDC-operated National Notifiable Disease Surveillance System.

intervention_and_participants_defined.quote: characteristics of 7755 patients with COVID-19 in South Korea as of 13 March 2020 using surveillance data retrieved from the KCDC-operated National Notifiable Disease Surveillance System.

outcome_assessed_for_60pct.quote: The female-to-male ratio was 62:38. The age group of 20–29 years accounted for 28.9% of all cases, followed by the age groups of 50–59 and 40–49 years.

follow_up_length_reported.quote: The median interval between onset of symptom and death was 10 days (range 1–24 days), while the median interval between date of hospitalisation and the date of death was 5 days (range 0–16 days).

outcome_assessors_blinded.quote: using surveillance data retrieved from the KCDC-operated National Notifiable Disease Surveillance System.

outcome_definition_objective_precise.quote: The case fatality proportion was 0.1% among the age groups of 30–39 and 40–49 years, then increased to 0.4% (50–59 years), 1.5% (60–69 years), 5.0% (70–79 years) and 8.5% (≥80 years).

important_prognostic_factors_accounted_f.quote: The case fatality proportion was 0.1% among the age groups of 30–39 and 40–49 years, then increased to 0.4% (50–59 years), 1.5% (60–69 years), 5.0% (70–79 years) and 8.5% (≥80 years).

analysis_described_and_effect_quantified.quote: They described the characteristics of 66 fatal patients with COVID-19 as of 12 March 2020.
